data.epi_content:
I couldn't focus on the class content at all, so I skipped it and started contemplating myself instead.

Actually, "contemplation" is too grand a word—they're just pointless thoughts.

Anyway, I came up with a new hypothesis: maybe I'm experiencing hallucinations and auditory delusions...!

Perhaps I was abducted by aliens and subjected to human experimentation, or maybe I unknowingly ingested cocaine.

It might be denial of reality, but this whole situation is absurd to begin with.

The idea that I'm possessed inside a novel I was reading is... well.

Maybe. Maybe someone accidentally sprinkled a bunch of "happy white powder" in my room.

Those occasional auditory hallucinations, or conversations with an imaginary friend—whatever you want to call them.

Sometimes it feels like this young lady is having conversations with me while living her normal life.

And while I feel a sense of unnaturalness or slight discomfort after time passes, when we're actually conversing, I don't even notice and just chat naturally.

It mostly happens in tense situations—when I'm trying to plan what to do next and feel overwhelmed, or when I'm being bullied.

Maybe it's because of the stress in those situations.

Anyway, the fact remains that I'm showing symptoms typical of drug addicts.

Those useless thoughts helped time pass quickly.

Before I knew it, the clock had moved past 10 and was pointing to 11.

The classroom was still quite peaceful.

It would be really nice if they just left me alone.

Some students looked surprised when they saw me, some whispered among themselves, and some looked at me like I was prey, but nothing had happened yet.

When I was reading the book I'd brought, it was easy enough to ignore the paper balls and trash thrown at me from behind.

Some even lightly tapped the back of my head, but it was all within the bounds of quiet harassment.

Someone did hit my head hard enough to make a "thwack" sound, but while a few students giggled, nobody else paid attention.

The teacher glanced at the scene, briefly closed his eyes, then opened them and continued with his boring lecture on the history of ancient magic.

There seemed to be only four or five students actually listening, but I suppose having even that many listeners matters.

It's probably not important to that teacher anyway.

He must have heard about how my family collapsed for absurd reasons, but he probably just thinks it's karma coming back to me.

Come to think of it, does any of this make sense?

A dragon burned down the mansion, some fanatics occupied the mine that produced most of our income, the woman I can barely call "mother" had her affair exposed and can't show her face in public, my father passed away as I mentioned before, and my older brother who was supposed to inherit the family is imprisoned for treason.

He might even be dead. No, he definitely is.

There is another younger brother, but he's only half-related and barely human, so the family is simply ruined.

And nobody finds this strange.

They just brush it off with nonsense like "you made too many enemies" or "it's divine punishment."

Well, that's fiction for you. If these events had happened gradually after my possession, I might have been able to deal with them or at least get some warning.

Unfortunately, everything happened at once—these implausible events, lacking any coherence, were just plot devices created to bring down the villain character that I am. And I possessed her after all this had already happened, which makes things quite difficult.

I don't see any way to navigate through this.

If there were even a glimmer of hope or a clue, I wouldn't have been trapped in that dark space unable to move, and I certainly wouldn't have played Russian Roulette where I might have died.
data.epi_content2:
Still, there's one fortunate thing in all this misfortune.

Well, it's not me who thinks it's fortunate, but rather this body.

The fact that I'm not in the same class as Vivian and Evan.

If those two found out I was being treated like this in class, I might die of shame.

I already died once.

After taking all sorts of useless drugs.

When the long class finally ended, lunchtime arrived.

Whether I wanted to eat lunch or just wanted to get out of the stuffy classroom, I don't know, but as I was about to leave, someone blocked my path.

Looking at her face, I didn't recognize her.

From her friendly smile mixed with an air of superiority and contempt, I could guess what was happening.

I turned my head slightly to the left and saw that the teacher had already left the classroom.

Looking around, I noticed most students were already leaving too.

The only ones properly seated were Lydia and the students listening to her.

I thought about running away, but this body is too weak and frail to even push aside the teenage girl in front of me.

Sometimes I even stagger while walking because I'm so drained of energy.

"Ha, ugh."

In the end, all I could do was let out a hollow laugh.

Turning around, I saw Lydia sitting in a corner with her legs crossed, laughing.

"Miss, why are you laughing?

I should be the one laughing, hee.

Oh, did you know we'd play a little before going to eat today?

Still, it's been so long since we've met, I'm a little hurt."

She pulled out a tin can with a match drawing on it and placed it on the desk.

With her slender fingers, she opened it just enough to see the hole inside, then tapped it lightly, causing a match with a white tip to pop up.

"Remember what you used to say all the time?

That people should be courteous and dignified.

What was it? Even if you become haggard and sickly, even if you're badly injured."

I must have been brainwashed with this education since childhood.

As Lydia spoke mockingly, I felt a slight fever in my head and found myself answering without thinking.

"...That's right."

"Isn't it discourteous not to come when a superior calls, hiding away in your room?"

"Superior?"

"Your family, well, the name remains but it's buried in debt. It'll be sold off soon.

Your mother is involved in some unsavory business, and your father escaped to the afterlife long ago.

So doesn't that make me your superior? You're nothing now, Miss."

She took out a match and waved it in front of me like she was showing off candy.

The smell was terrible.
data.epi_content3:
A peculiar odor that's hard to describe, something like burnt garlic.

"We were all taught basic magic here, so we never had to use or see these things, right?

But commoners use these matches to light candles or torches.

I thought I'd show you in case you have to use them after leaving the Academy."

She lightly brushed the tip against the desk, which shouldn't have been rough enough to cause friction, yet it burst into flame.

Lydia made a sound like "ah-hot" that might seem cute to some, then threw the match on the floor and stomped on it.

"Girls, open the Miss's mouth.

If she tries to scream from pain, don't block her mouth and risk getting your fingers cut off—just knock her out and use some healing magic."

Suddenly the atmosphere shifted from nasty bullying to something more like a noir film where they might beat someone up and dump them in the sea.

Perhaps it means they don't care what happens as long as I don't die.

Right, in this world where healing magic can restore you to normal even if you're reduced to a piece of meat, as long as you're still alive, maybe they've become numb to such things.

Two girls standing on either side approached me. One grabbed my lower jaw while pressing down with her other hand.

The other girl held my upper lip as if covering my philtrum and applied pressure.

No matter how hard I tried to close my mouth, I couldn't.

"Ah, I'd be a little sad if you quit because of this.

You have nowhere to go, hee. At our age, if you went out on the streets... hmm.

Since you were raised being loved and pampered, you could probably become a high-class prostitute right away, couldn't you?"

The match ignites.

It shows a white light, then emits acrid, smelly smoke before burning red.

And Lydia, playfully, as if throwing a dart, tossed the burning match into my mouth.

Is there anything more to explain here?

My mouth was cooked to medium-well, and thanks to the match going down my throat, every time I swallow saliva or breathe, it burns insanely.

I twist my body trying to escape even though there's no way out, my consciousness becomes slightly blurred, tears cloud my vision and I feel like I'm about to pass out, white foam forms in my throat, I feel violently nauseous, my trembling legs suddenly give out, I feel a sense of shame as I might have wet myself from the pain, Lydia is laughing and saying something in front of me but I can't hear anything, someone is kicking my stomach lightly while laughing and they look terrifyingly insane, and just... well... that's it.

The group surrounding me must have left to actually go eat lunch.

There really is no way out of this.

I have no money.

No abilities.

No acquaintances.

No strength.

The only options that come to mind are becoming a prostitute or somehow graduating from here...

But what would I have after graduation?

Why am I clinging to the Academy?

It doesn't seem like anything would change even if I graduate.

I could maybe become a librarian.

But is that what I want to do with my life?

I don't know.
